description_long: This is a roughly rectangular, block-shaped stone with softly rounded edges and a matte, sandy-brown surface. The stone appears solid and dense, with a natural, unpolished texture that suggests it was shaped by weathering rather than by tools. One side of the stone features a dark marking or inscription, possibly graffiti or a natural mineral deposit, which stands out against the otherwise uniform coloration. The surface is irregular, with subtle undulations and a granular appearance typical of sedimentary rock. The stone's size and shape make it suitable as a low barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, such as a park or sidewalk. Its overall appearance is utilitarian, blending into both natural and built settings.
description: A blocky, sandy-brown stone with rounded edges and a matte, granular surface, featuring a dark marking on one side.
description_view_0: The stone's broad, flat top and gently rounded edges are visible, showing its sandy-brown, uniform texture.
description_view_1: This view highlights a dark marking or inscription on one side, contrasting with the otherwise plain surface.
description_view_2: The stone's side is visible, showing its irregular, granular surface without any distinct markings.
description_view_3: The opposite end of the stone is shown, with a smooth, unmarked surface and softly rounded corners.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or used as a low barrier
attribute_pattern: Mostly uniform granular texture with a single dark marking or inscription on one side; no regular veining or layering.
attribute_weathering: Surface appears naturally weathered, with rounded edges and a matte, slightly rough finish, indicating moderate exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends subtly with natural ground surfaces or landscaped urban areas, but the dark marking may draw some visual attention.
